target.name: Orangewood Labs
target.founder: Abhinav Das, Aditya Bhatia, Akash Bansal

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $105M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

data.narrative:
Orangewood Labs is a Series A robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $5M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 9 private companies and 1 public company operating in the same sector and stage cohort, including peers such as 1X Technologies, Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, Collaborative Robotics, and Fairmat.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised ($105M proxy) and a typical Series A step-up multiplier of 2.0x, yields $210M. Peer-median analysis across six same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables produces a median valuation of $1.1B, with a range spanning $300M to $10.0B. The dispersion reflects heterogeneity in robotics subsector and maturity; the lower quartile (Collaborative Robotics, Fairmat) clusters around $350M–$500M, while later-stage outliers (1X Technologies at $10.0B) skew the distribution.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $178M (low) to $1.2B (high), with a median of $642M. The wide spread reflects two material uncertainties: the stage-progression method assumes modest growth from current capitalization, while the peer-median approach captures significant variance in business model, capital efficiency, and market positioning within robotics. Key risks include peer-set stage heterogeneity (most comparables are Series B) and sector volatility. A defensible midpoint estimate of $600M–$700M balances these signals, though placement within the range depends on product differentiation and near-term funding trajectory.
